selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 10.5% and 20-day momentum is 13.4%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 8.3% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.6%, downside volatility near 1.2%, and a recent max drawdown of 5.2%.
Volume confirmation is 12.2% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 11.5% versus SPY over 5 days and 3.6% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.82, confidence 0.77, and 0.6 weighted articles across 1 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.82, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 2 articles across 1 sources, 0.6 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.81, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.77.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Overall model score is 0.247 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Beats Lawsuit Alleging that Talc caused Three Women’s Cancer
news_evidence.llm_reason: Article reports a favorable jury finding in a talc-related lawsuit against JNJ — a company-specific legal development that reduces litigation overhang and uncertainty. This is material for investor sentiment and potential liability expectations, likely modestly positive for the stock over the next 1–10 trading days.
